page: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a virtual machine within the Cloud GPU family, featuring 2 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, an 80 GB local NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU providing 4 GB of dedicated VRAM. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. The shared Intel processor delivers weak multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3512.53 and a weak PassMark CPU score of 3681.75. Memory bandwidth is also relatively low, measuring 28.99 GB/sec for large block reads. However, the integrated NVIDIA A16 GPU partition excels at small-scale parallel workloads, achieving top-tier LLM inference prompt processing speeds of 8765.82 tokens/sec for a 135M model and 308.92 tokens/sec for a 7B model.

This instance offers a cost-effective entry point for GPU-accelerated computing by utilizing fractional hardware resources. By bundling a quarter of an NVIDIA A16 GPU and utilizing shared CPU cores, it lowers the financial barrier to hardware-accelerated workloads compared to dedicated GPU instances. The primary tradeoff is the limited CPU processing power and database performance, as evidenced by a poor PassMark database score of 1005.99.

The Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is suited for lightweight machine learning inference, small LLM prompt processing, development environments for GPU applications, and basic media transcoding. It is less suitable for heavy multi-threaded CPU tasks, high-throughput database operations, or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance designed for lightweight accelerated workloads. It features 2 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, an 80 GB NVMe SSD, and a 0.25x NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 4 GB of VRAM. While CPU-bound benchmarks show weak multi-core and database performance, the instance delivers top-tier results in LLM prompt processing, reaching 308.92 tokens/sec on a 7B model. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for developers needing GPU acceleration without the expense of a dedicated card. It is best suited for small-scale machine learning inference, GPU application development, and basic media processing.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a fractional GPU virtual server featuring 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, an 80 GB NVMe SSD, and a 0.25x NVIDIA A16 GPU with 4 GB VRAM for entry-level accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a fractional GPU server with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 0.25x NVIDIA A16 GPU for lightweight inference.
tagline: An entry-level fractional GPU instance pairing shared Intel processors with NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for cost-effective machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 4 GB of dedicated VRAM
2 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLMs
Best suited for lightweight machine learning inference and GPU development
